# Implementation Plan: Cub Scout Volunteer Management Webapp

**Branch**: `001-volunteer-management` | **Date**: 2026-03-12 | **Spec**: [spec.md](spec.md)
**Input**: Feature specification from `/specs/001-volunteer-management/spec.md`

**Note**: This plan is generated by the `/speckit.plan` command following the BDD-first constitution.

## Summary

Build a full-stack web application to manage Cub Scout volunteer activities with gamification system to incentivize parent participation and simplify administrative task management. The system implements three-tier authorization (parent/guardian volunteers, den leaders/committee members, and site admins), volunteer event coordination, points-based gamification with badge levels, administrative task tracking, and comprehensive reporting capabilities.

**Technical Approach**: Separate Next.js frontend with Node.js/Express backend API using Prisma ORM with SQLite database. RESTful API communication via Axios. Test-first development with Jest/Vitest for unit tests, Playwright for E2E tests, and contract testing for API endpoints.

## Technical Context

**Language/Version**: Node.js 20.x LTS (backend), TypeScript 5.x (both frontend and backend)
**Primary Dependencies**: 
- Backend: Express.js, Prisma ORM, bcrypt (password hashing), jsonwebtoken (auth), zod (validation)
- Frontend: Next.js 14.x (App Router), React 18, Axios, TailwindCSS, shadcn/ui components
**Storage**: SQLite database (via Prisma ORM)
**Testing**: Jest (backend unit tests), Vitest + React Testing Library (frontend), Playwright (E2E), Supertest (API contract tests)
**Target Platform**: Web browsers (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge), mobile-responsive
**Project Type**: Full-stack web application (separate frontend + backend)
**Performance Goals**: <3s page load on mobile, <1s API response time for standard queries, handle 50-100 concurrent users
**Constraints**: Mobile-responsive required, 80%+ code coverage, offline-capable not required but graceful degradation needed
**Scale/Scope**: Single pack deployment (50-100 volunteers), 500-1000 events per year, 10-20 administrative tasks

## Constitution Check

*GATE: Must pass before Phase 0 research. Re-check after Phase 1 design.*

### I. Behavior-Driven Development (BDD First) ✅ PASS

- **Specification Format**: All 10 user stories written in Given-When-Then format with clear acceptance criteria
- **Test-First Workflow**: Development will follow Red-Green-Refactor cycle with tests written before implementation
- **Contract Tests**: API endpoints will have contract tests (using Supertest) before implementing handlers
- **Integration Tests**: E2E tests (using Playwright) will verify complete user journeys per spec
- **Independent Testability**: Each user story is independently testable as defined in spec

**Status**: ✅ No violations. Specification follows BDD format. Implementation plan requires test-first development.

### II. Clean and Well-Documented Code ✅ PASS

- **Single Responsibility**: Backend API routes separated from business logic (services layer pattern)
- **Clear Naming**: TypeScript enforces type safety; ESLint configured for consistent naming conventions
- **Documentation Requirements**: 
  - JSDoc comments for all public APIs
  - README files for backend/, frontend/, and major subsystems
  - API documentation via OpenAPI/Swagger spec
  - Code comments explaining "why" for complex business rules (points calculation, authorization logic)
- **Maintainability**: TypeScript provides type safety, reducing reliance on comments for "what"

**Status**: ✅ No violations. Plan includes documentation requirements and clean architecture patterns.

### III. DRY (Don't Repeat Yourself) ✅ PASS

- **Shared Types**: Prisma schema as single source of truth for data models; TypeScript types generated from schema
- **API Contracts**: Define request/response types once, share between frontend and backend via shared package
- **Business Rules**: 
  - Points calculation logic centralized in backend service layer
  - Authorization tier logic centralized in auth middleware
  - Badge tier thresholds defined in configuration (not hardcoded)
- **Configuration**: Environment variables for database URL, JWT secrets, port configurations
- **Validation**: Zod schemas define validation rules once, reused across API endpoints

**Status**: ✅ No violations. Architecture enforces single source of truth for data models, business rules, and configurations.

### Overall Assessment

**PASSED** - No constitutional violations identified. Proceed with Phase 0 research.

## Project Structure

### Documentation (this feature)

```text
specs/[###-feature]/
├── plan.md              # This file (/speckit.plan command output)
├── research.md          # Phase 0 output (/speckit.plan command)
├── data-model.md        # Phase 1 output (/speckit.plan command)
├── quickstart.md        # Phase 1 output (/speckit.plan command)
├── contracts/           # Phase 1 output (/speckit.plan command)
└── tasks.md             # Phase 2 output (/speckit.tasks command - NOT created by /speckit.plan)
```

### Source Code (repository root)

```text
backend/
├── prisma/
│   ├── schema.prisma          # Data models (single source of truth)
│   ├── migrations/            # Database migrations
│   └── seed.ts                # Seed data for development
├── src/
│   ├── models/                # Prisma client types, data access layers
│   ├── services/              # Business logic (points calculation, role management)
│   ├── middleware/            # Auth, validation, error handling
│   ├── api/                   # Express routes
│   │   ├── auth.routes.ts     # Authentication endpoints
│   │   ├── volunteers.routes.ts
│   │   ├── events.routes.ts
│   │   ├── tasks.routes.ts
│   │   ├── points.routes.ts
│   │   └── reports.routes.ts
│   ├── config/                # Environment config, constants
│   ├── utils/                 # Helpers (validation schemas, formatters)
│   └── index.ts               # Express app entry point
├── tests/
│   ├── contract/              # Supertest API contract tests
│   ├── integration/           # Multi-service integration tests
│   └── unit/                  # Jest unit tests for services
├── package.json
├── tsconfig.json
└── .env.example

frontend/
├── src/
│   ├── app/                   # Next.js 14 App Router
│   │   ├── layout.tsx         # Root layout
│   │   ├── page.tsx           # Home page (dashboard)
│   │   ├── auth/              # Authentication pages
│   │   ├── events/            # Event management pages
│   │   ├── tasks/             # Administrative tasks pages
│   │   ├── leaderboard/       # Gamification pages
│   │   └── reports/           # Reporting pages
│   ├── components/
│   │   ├── ui/                # shadcn/ui components
│   │   ├── forms/             # Form components with validation
│   │   ├── layouts/           # Layout components (header, nav, footer)
│   │   └── shared/            # Reusable components (cards, modals, tables)
│   ├── services/              # Axios API client, auth service
│   ├── hooks/                 # Custom React hooks
│   ├── lib/                   # Utilities, shared types
│   └── styles/                # TailwindCSS, global styles
├── tests/
│   ├── e2e/                   # Playwright E2E tests
│   └── unit/                  # Vitest + React Testing Library tests
├── public/                    # Static assets
├── package.json
├── tsconfig.json
└── .env.local.example

shared/                        # Optional: shared types package
├── types/
│   ├── api.types.ts           # API request/response types
│   ├── models.types.ts        # Generated from Prisma
│   └── validation.types.ts    # Zod schemas
└── package.json
```

**Structure Decision**: Web application with separate backend and frontend. Backend uses Express.js with Prisma ORM for data access and service layer for business logic. Frontend uses Next.js 14 App Router with component-based architecture. Optional shared package for type definitions to enforce DRY principle across frontend/backend boundary.

## Planning Artifacts Generated

### Phase 0: Research ✅ COMPLETE

**Output**: [research.md](research.md)

**Key Decisions**:
1. **Prisma Schema Patterns**: Soft deletes with `deletedAt` timestamp, CUID primary keys, strategic indexing for SQLite
2. **Next.js Auth Patterns**: JWT session management with HttpOnly cookies, middleware for protected routes, Axios interceptors
3. **Express Auth & RBAC**: JWT middleware with tier-based authorization, bcrypt (12 rounds), cryptographically secure password reset
4. **Gamification Architecture**: Immutable event sourcing for points, cached leaderboard with incremental updates, threshold-based badge tiers

### Phase 1: Design & Contracts ✅ COMPLETE

**Outputs**:
- [data-model.md](data-model.md) - Complete Prisma schema with 13 entities, relationships, validation rules
- [contracts/auth-api.md](contracts/auth-api.md) - Authentication endpoints (register, login, reset password)
- [contracts/volunteers-api.md](contracts/volunteers-api.md) - Volunteer profile and role management
- [contracts/events-api.md](contracts/events-api.md) - Event creation, signups, completion
- [contracts/points-api.md](contracts/points-api.md) - Points, leaderboard, badge tiers
- [contracts/admin-tasks-api.md](contracts/admin-tasks-api.md) - Administrative task management
- [contracts/reports-config-api.md](contracts/reports-config-api.md) - Reporting and pack configuration
- [quickstart.md](quickstart.md) - Developer setup guide with environment config, seed data, API examples
- `.github/agents/copilot-instructions.md` - Updated with technology stack context

**Entity Model Summary**:
- Core: Volunteer, VolunteerRole, ChildRank, VolunteerToRole
- Events: Event, ActivityType, ActivitySlot, Signup
- Gamification: PointEvent, VolunteerPointBalance, LeaderboardCache, BadgeTier, BadgeTierHistory
- Tasks: AdminTask, AdminTaskToRole, TaskCompletion
- Config: PackConfig, Notification, PasswordReset, AuditLog

**API Surface**:
- 42 REST endpoints across 6 domain areas
- 3 authorization tiers enforced consistently
- Zod validation schemas for all inputs
- Comprehensive error handling documented
